messages.content:
In case of gastrointestinal diseases diet is characterized by following


a.
Small and frequent meals


b.
Large and rare meals


c.
Fluids and foods should be consumed when they are of a moderate temperature


d.
Fluids and foods should be consumed when they are hot

a. Small and frequent meals

c. Fluids and foods should be consumed when they are of a moderate temperature

The amount of energy used for basal metabolism in a period of time is called


a.
basal metabolic rate


b.
coefficient of physical activity


c.
dietary-induced thermogenesis


d.
physical activity level
a. basal metabolic rate

The group of non-microbial food poisoning includes: 


a.
poisoning with products that are poisonous by nature


b.
toxicosis


c.
chemical poisoning


d.
poisoning with products that are poisonous under certain conditions


e.
toxic infections (toxicoinfection)

a. poisoning with products that are poisonous by nature

c. chemical poisoning

d. poisoning with products that are poisonous under certain conditions

Choose the most common symptom of vitamin C deficiency: 


a.
conjunctivitis and blepharitis


b.
night blindness


c.
follicular hyperkeratosis (goosebumps) in combination with petechiae


d.
corneal vascularization


e.
burning sensation in the feet
c. follicular hyperkeratosis (goosebumps) in combination with petechiae

Classification of non microbial food poisoning  includes:


a.
phycotoxycosis


b.
poisoning caused by endocrine glands of animals (adrenal, pancreas)


c.
poisoning caused by fungi toxins


d.
poisoning caused by canned food containing botulinum toxin

a. phycotoxycosis

c. poisoning caused by fungi toxins

d. poisoning caused by canned food containing botulinum toxin

The metabolic response to food increases total energy expenditure by


a.
about 1 % of the BMR over a 24-hour period in individuals eating a mixed diet


b.
about 78% of the BMR over a 24-hour period in individuals eating a mixed diet


c.
about 50% of the BMR over a 24-hour period in individuals eating a mixed diet


d.
about 10 % of the BMR over a 24-hour period in individuals eating a mixed diet
a. about 1 % of the BMR over a 24-hour period in individuals eating a mixed diet

Minerals and vitamins provide


a.
0 Calories/gm


b.
0.9 Calories/gm


c.
9 Calories/gm


d.
4 Calories/gm
a. 0 Calories/gm

According to the law of the energetic adequacy of nutrition 


a.
food must be cooked in accordance to the digestive system enzymic abilities


b.
the dietary intake food calorific value must  correspond to the organism energy consumption


c.
food must not be toxic and must be harmless in epidemiological aspect


d.
food intake hours must correspond to the organism biological rhythms
b. the dietary intake food calorific value must correspond to the organism energy consumption

Food poisoning is characterized by:


a.
infectivity, seasonality


b.
relationship all diseased with one food


c.
territorial limitations of disease case, do not contagious


d.
the mass character, contagious


e.
sudden onset, short incubation period
e. sudden onset, short incubation period

Vitamin A deficiency can cause:


a.
nervous disorders, hypothermia


b.
anaemia, rickets 


c.
xeophthalmia, stunted growth


d.
beri-beri disease


e.
night blindness

c. xerophthalmia, stunted growth

e. night blindness

The main functions of food are:


a.
Maintenance and regulation of tissue functions


b.
Body building and repair


c.
Provision of energy

a. Maintenance and regulation of tissue functions

b. Body building and repair

c. Provision of energy

Vitamin deficiency can be the cause of:


a.
caries


b.
beri-beri, scurvy


c.
rachitis, osteomalacia


d.
endemic goiter

a. caries

b. beri-beri, scurvy

c. rachitis, osteomalacia

d. endemic goiter

Deficiency of fluoride can be the cause of:


a.
endemic goiter


b.
fluorosis


c.
gout


d.
caries


e.
coronary artery disease

d. caries

b. fluorosis

According to the law of enzymatic adequacy of nutrition:


a.
food intake hours must correspond to the organism biological rhythms


b.
food must not be toxic and must be harmless in epidemiological aspect


c.
the chemical composition of the food must correspond to enzyme systems of the body


d.
the dietary intake food calorific value must correspond to the organism energy consumption
c. the chemical composition of the food must correspond to enzyme systems of the body

Classification of food poisoning includes


a.
non-microbial food poisonings


b.
food poisonings caused by plant


c.
microbial food poisonings


d.
unknown etiology


e.
viral food poisonings

a. non-microbial food poisonings

c. microbial food poisonings

e. viral food poisonings

Classification of microbial food poisoning includes:


a.
poisoning caused by poisonous fish


b.
poisoning caused by nitrites and nitrates


c.
poisoning caused by phycotoxins


d.
foodborne diseases 


e.
mycotoxicosis

d. foodborne diseases

e. mycotoxicosis

Alimentary diseases are caused by:


a.
imbalance, insufficient or excess of essential nutrients in the diet


b.
exogenous causes, disease


c.
primary chronic (rarely - acute) lack of nutrients


d.
primary acute (rarely - chronic) lack of nutrients
a. imbalance, insufficient or excess of essential nutrients in the diet

Carbohydrate provides


a.
5 Calories/gm,


b.
4 Calories/gm


c.
14 Calories/gm


d.
9 Calories/gm,
b. 4 Calories/gm

Human needs energy for the following:


a.
Physical and mental activity


b.
Specific dynamic action of food


c.
Basal metabolism

a. Physical and mental activity

b. Specific dynamic action of food 

c. Basal metabolism

Fat requirements


a.
33 g. fat/Mcal


b.
9 g. fat/Mcal


c.
56 g. fat/Mcal


d.
145 g. fat/Mcal
a. 33 g. fat/Mcal

Basics of clinical nutrition are following:


a.
using methods of training, reducing diets and contrast days


b.
correspondence of the chemical structure of food to the enzyme system


c.
food intake should be about 5–6 times per day


d.
to provide sick person with nutrients according to physiological requirements


e.
food intake should be about 2–3 times per day

b. correspondence of the chemical structure of food to the enzyme system

d. to provide sick person with nutrients according to physiological requirements

Correlate the disease and its definition:

Alimentary dystrophy (starvation disease)

Answer 1
Choose...
Kwashiorkor  

Answer 2
Choose...
Protein-energy malnutrition (PEM)

Answer 3
Choose...
Nutritional marasmus
Answer 3 - Nutritional marasmus

According to the law of the biorhythmological adequacy of nutrition 


a.
all nutrients must be contained in optimal quantities and ratios


b.
food intake hours must correspond to the organism biological rhythms


c.
the dietary intake food calorific value must correspond to the organism energy consumption


d.
food must not contain the etiological agents of infectious foodborne diseases
b. food intake hours must correspond to the organism biological rhythms

Functions of vitamin A:


a.
necessary for maintenance of resistance of blood capillaries membrane


b.
it helps in the production of retinol pigments needed for vision in darkness

light;


c.
body growth


d.
helps form and maintain healthy epithelial tissues

tissues;


e.
supports immune function

b. it helps in the production of retinol pigments needed for vision in darkness

d. helps form and maintain healthy epithelial tissues

e. supports immune function
Water soluble vitamins are
Vitamins B and C

Types of insufficient nutritional status are:


a.
insufficient subcompensated


b.
insufficient compensated


c.
insufficient morbid


d.
insufficient defective


e.
insufficient premorbid

b. insufficient compensated

c. insufficient morbid

d. insufficient defective

Fats serve the following functions:


a.
provide support for inner organs


b.
source of energy


c.
maintaining  body temperature


d.
carriers of fat-soluble vitamins


e.
carriers of water-soluble vitamins and minerals

a. provide support for inner organs

b. source of energy

c. maintaining body temperature

d. carriers of fat-soluble vitamins

Carbohydrate requirements


a.
30 g. carbohydrate /Mcal


b.
33 g. carbohydrate /Mcal


c.
145 g. carbohydrate /Mcal


d.
4 g. carbohydrate /Mcal
b. 33 g. carbohydrate /Mcal
